Architecture & Custom Backends

Source: vignettes/extending-tlframe.Rmd
extending-tlframe.Rmd

This guide covers tlframe’s internal architecture. It is intended for developers who want to understand the render pipeline, write custom backends, or contribute to the package.

Architecture Overview

tlframe is organised into three layers across 24 source modules:

+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| API Layer (10 files)                                                 |
| fr_table -> fr_cols -> fr_header -> fr_titles -> ... -> fr_render    |
| Each verb: validate -> modify fr_spec -> return fr_spec              |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Infrastructure Layer (9 files)                                       |
| classes.R     S3 constructors (fr_spec, fr_col, fr_rule, ...)        |
| columns.R     Width estimation, AFM metrics, auto-distribution       |
| config.R      YAML discovery, loading, merging                       |
| constants.R   fr_env: fonts, paper sizes, presets, colors            |
| helpers.R     Error conditions, text normalisation                   |
| markup.R      Inline rich text: fr_super() -> sentinel tokens        |
| tokens.R      {thepage}, {program}, {datetime} resolution            |
| units.R       Twips, half-points, row height calculation             |
| validate.R    Type-safe validation helpers                           |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Render Layer (5 files)                                               |
| render.R          Dispatch: finalize -> prepare_pages -> backend     |
| render-common.R   Shared: cell grid, borders, escaping, colors       |
| render-rtf.R      RTF 1.9.1 backend                                  |
| render-latex.R    tabularray/XeLaTeX backend                         |
| render-pdf.R      PDF wrapper (generates .tex, compiles twice)       |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+

The fr_spec object

Every pipeline verb reads and writes to a single S3 object, fr_spec. Its key fields:

Field Type Set by
$data data.frame fr_table()
$meta$titles list of title entries fr_titles()
$meta$footnotes list of footnote entries fr_footnotes()
$columns named list of fr_col fr_cols()
$columns_meta list (split, width_mode) fr_cols()
$header list (n, format, bold, …) fr_header()
$body list (page_by, group_by, …) fr_rows()
$rules list of fr_rule fr_hlines(), fr_vlines()
$cell_styles list of fr_cell_style fr_styles()
$page list (orientation, margins, …) fr_page()
$pagehead list (left, center, right) fr_pagehead()
$pagefoot list (left, center, right) fr_pagefoot()
$spacing list (titles_after, …) fr_spacing()

The fr_env environment

fr_env (in constants.R) is a private package environment holding all lookup data and mutable state:

# Font families and their AFM font names
names(fr_backends())
#> [1] "format"      "extensions"  "description"

# Paper sizes are stored internally
pg <- fr_get_page(tbl_demog |> fr_table())
pg$paper
#> [1] "letter"

Key contents: $fonts (family → AFM mapping), $paper_sizes (name → dimensions), $hline_presets (name → rule set), $named_colors (148 CSS colors), $unicode_map (symbol → codepoint), $liberation_fallback (system font → Liberation font).

The Render Pipeline

When fr_render(spec, path) is called, it runs a three-stage pipeline:

fr_spec
  |
  v
finalize_spec()            <- Resolve all deferred computations
  |  - Build default columns for unmentioned data columns
  |  - Distribute widths (auto/fit/equal/percent)
  |  - Resolve N-count header labels
  |  - Insert blank_after rows
  |  - Apply indent_by offsets
  |  - Compute decimal geometry for align="decimal" columns
  |
  v
prepare_pages()            <- Split data into page groups
  |  - Split by page_by column values (preserving data order)
  |  - Each group: {label, data, n_counts}
  |
  v
calculate_col_panels()     <- Handle wide tables
  |  - If .split=TRUE and total width > page: split into panels
  |  - Stub columns repeat in each panel
  |  - Scale panel widths proportionally
  |
  v
render_<format>()          <- Backend-specific rendering
     RTF:   render_rtf(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)
     LaTeX: render_latex(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)
     PDF:   render_pdf() -> render_latex() + xelatex compilation

finalize_spec: The resolution stage

This is where deferred computations are resolved. Key operations:

Width distribution: If .width = "auto", column widths are estimated from content using AFM font metrics. If total width exceeds the printable area, columns are scaled down proportionally:

spec <- tbl_demog |>
  fr_table() |>
  fr_cols(.width = "auto", group = fr_col(visible = FALSE))

# Widths are resolved at render time, not when fr_cols() is called
# This allows verb-order independence

N-count labels: fr_header(n = ..., format = "{name}\n(N={n})") stores the N-counts and format template. finalize_spec() resolves them into final column labels so that fr_cols() and fr_header() can be called in any order.

Blank rows: blank_after = "group" inserts empty rows after each group boundary. group_by auto-implies blank_after on the same column.

Decimal geometry: For align = "decimal" columns, the 15-type stat display engine analyses each cell value, detects its type (count, float, n/%, CI, p-value, range, etc.), and computes space-padded alignment offsets.

prepare_pages: The splitting stage

When page_by = "param" is set, data is split by unique(data$param) (preserving original data order, not alphabetical). Each page group is rendered as a separate section.

calculate_col_panels: The width stage

When .split = TRUE and the total column width exceeds the printable area, columns are distributed across panels. Stub columns repeat in every panel. Each panel’s widths are scaled proportionally to fill the page.

Writing a Custom Backend

tlframe’s backend interface is formalised. Any backend must implement a single function with this signature:

render_<format>(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)

Arguments:

Argument Type Description
spec Finalised fr_spec All deferred computations resolved
page_groups List of lists Each: $label, $data, $n_counts
col_panels List of char vectors Column name groups per panel
path Character scalar Output file path

Return: The output file path (character scalar).

Registering a backend 

# Register a custom backend for HTML output
render_html <- function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) {
  # ... generate HTML ...
  writeLines(html, path)
  path
}

fr_register_backend("html", render_html, extensions = "html")

# Now this works:
spec |> fr_render("output.html")

Shared utilities in render-common.R

The render layer provides shared utilities that any backend can use:

build_cell_grid() — Converts the data frame into a matrix of rendered cell content, applying inline markup resolution and decimal alignment.

build_header_cell_grid() — Same for the header row, with N-count labels already resolved.

resolve_borders() — Converts the $rules list into a per-cell border specification (top, bottom, left, right for each cell).

collect_colors() — Scans all styles and backgrounds to build a color table (RTF needs a \colortbl; LaTeX needs \definecolor preamble).

rtf_escape_and_resolve() / latex_escape_and_resolve() — Escape special characters and resolve markup sentinels (\x01SUPER:a\x02 → {\super a} or \textsuperscript{a}).

The Markup Sentinel System

Inline markup (fr_super(), fr_bold(), etc.) works through a sentinel token system that survives string concatenation:

User writes:       "P{fr_super('a')}-value < 0.05"
                          ↓
glue evaluation:   "P\x01SUPER:a\x02-value < 0.05"
                          ↓
RTF backend:       "P{\super a}-value < 0.05"
LaTeX backend:     "P\textsuperscript{a}-value < 0.05"

The \x01...\x02 sentinel tokens are non-printable bytes that:

  1. Survive paste(), sprintf(), and other string operations
  2. Are invisible in console output
  3. Are resolved per-backend at the final rendering stage

This design means markup works in titles, footnotes, column labels, and data cells — anywhere a string is accepted.

Supported sentinel types

Type Sentinel RTF LaTeX
Superscript \x01SUPER:x\x02 {\super x} \textsuperscript{x}
Subscript \x01SUB:x\x02 {\sub x} \textsubscript{x}
Bold \x01BOLD:x\x02 {\b x} \textbf{x}
Italic \x01ITALIC:x\x02 {\i x} \textit{x}
Underline \x01UNDERLINE:x\x02 {\ul x} \underline{x}
Newline \x01NEWLINE\x02 \line \newline
Unicode \x01UNICODE:code\x02 \u<code>? Direct UTF-8

AFM Font Metrics

tlframe uses real Adobe Font Metrics for accurate column width estimation. Pre-parsed metrics for 12 font variants are stored in R/sysdata.rda:

Family Regular Bold Italic Bold Italic
Helvetica Helvetica Helvetica-Bold Helvetica-Oblique Helvetica-BoldOblique
Times Times-Roman Times-Bold Times-Italic Times-BoldItalic
Courier Courier Courier-Bold Courier-Oblique Courier-BoldOblique

Width ratios illustrate why AFM matters:

  • Helvetica ‘M’ = 833 units vs ‘i’ = 222 units (3.75x ratio)
  • Courier is fixed at 600 units for all characters
  • A “Summary Statistics” column in Helvetica needs different width than in Courier, and tlframe accounts for this automatically

The estimate_col_width() function measures actual text widths (both header labels and data content) using measure_text_width_twips(), which sums per-character AFM widths for the correct font variant.

CI/CD and Docker

Batch rendering in CI 

# render_all.R — run in CI pipeline
library(tlframe)
fr_config("_tlframe.yml")

metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {
  row <- metadata[i, ]
  data <- get(row$data_name)
  recipe <- readRDS(sprintf("recipes/%s.rds", row$recipe))

  data |>
    fr_table() |>
    fr_apply(recipe) |>
    fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1) |>
    fr_render(sprintf("output/Table_%s.rtf",
                      gsub("\\.", "_", row$table_id)))
}

Docker setup

For reproducible PDF rendering in containers:

FROM rocker/r-ver:4.3.3

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libxml2-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev

# Install R packages
RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','glue','rlang','tidyselect','vctrs','yaml','stringi'))"
RUN R -e "install.packages('remotes'); remotes::install_github('vthanik/tlframe')"

# For PDF output: install TinyTeX
RUN R -e "install.packages('tinytex'); tinytex::install_tinytex()"

# Liberation fonts are bundled with tlframe — no Microsoft fonts needed
# tlframe auto-detects and uses Liberation as fallback on Linux

No Microsoft fonts required in the container — tlframe’s bundled Liberation fonts provide pixel-compatible rendering for Courier New, Arial, and Times New Roman.

Validation in CI 

# validate_all.R — run after rendering
library(tlframe)

metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
errors <- character(0)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {
  row <- metadata[i, ]
  data <- get(row$data_name)
  recipe <- readRDS(sprintf("recipes/%s.rds", row$recipe))

  spec <- data |>
    fr_table() |>
    fr_apply(recipe) |>
    fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1)

  result <- fr_validate(spec, strict = FALSE)
  if (!result$valid) {
    errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Table %s: %s",
                                 row$table_id,
                                 paste(result$messages, collapse = "; ")))
  }
}

if (length(errors) > 0) {
  stop("Validation failures:\n", paste(errors, collapse = "\n"))
}

On this page

Developed by Vignesh Thanikachalam.
